channel.title: GeoPulse. Geopolitics in brief
channel.description: Signal-first briefings on geopolitics, edited by Lavkesh Dwivedi.

item.title: Musk testifies at OpenAI trial it’s not OK to ‘loot a charity’
item.description: Elon Musk testified at the OpenAI trial, stating it's not acceptable for the charity to become a profit-seeking firm. He claims OpenAI cofounder and president betrayed him and the public in the process.

item.title: Amid Iran War and Tensions with Neighbors, U.A.E. Goes Its Own Way
item.description: The United Arab Emirates left OPEC, charting its own course amid tensions with Saudi Arabia and the Iran war. This decision has rocked the region. The United Arab Emirates’ decision to leave OPEC has rocked the region, underscoring how the country, at odds with Saudi Arabia, is increasingly charting its own course.

item.title: As US-Iran talks remain ‘stalled’, experts warn of ‘long-term disruptions’
item.description: US-Iran talks remain stalled, leading to soaring oil prices. Experts warn of long-term disruptions due to the ongoing standoff between the two sides. With the two sides locked in a standoff, oil prices have continued to soar.

item.title: Former FBI director Comey again indicted by US Justice Department
item.description: Former FBI director James Comey has been indicted by the US Justice Department for the second time. The move comes after Trump repeatedly called for his prosecution over the 2016 campaign investigation.

item.title: The biggest military spenders
item.description: Global military spending has increased for the 11th consecutive year, according to SIPRI's latest report. The growth marks another year of rising military expenditure worldwide. Global military spending is up again, according to SIPRI’s latest report showing the 11th consecutive year of growth.

item.title: U.S. Marines board merchant ship in Arabian Sea suspected to be headed to Iran
item.description: U. S. Marines boarded a merchant ship in the Arabian Sea, suspected of heading to Iran, but released it after a search confirmed no Iranian port call. The incident occurred without incident, with CENTCOM stating the ship was allowed to continue its voyage.

item.title: UN Sanctions Brother of Sudan's RSF Leader, Colombian Mercenaries
item.description: The UN has imposed sanctions on the brother of Sudan's RSF leader and Colombian mercenaries. The move targets those involved in the Sudan conflict and aims to curb their influence.

item.title: King Charles calls for NATO unity, Ukraine support in US Congress speech
item.description: King Charles called for NATO unity and Ukraine support in a speech to the US Congress, highlighting national friendship but also delivering pointed messages during his four-day US visit. The UK sovereign's remarks were seen as a gesture of solidarity with Western allies.

item.title: Armed group announces siege on Mali capital
item.description: JNIM announced a total siege on Mali's capital Bamako. JNIM has announced the beginning of a “total siege” on Mali’s capital Bamako. Armed group announces siege on Mali capital.

item.title: Trump claims Iran 'just' informed U.S. it's in 'state of collapse'
item.description: Donald Trump claimed that Iran told the US it is in a "state of collapse". Ceasefire efforts appear stalled, with Iran resisting US attempts to dictate terms. Ceasefire efforts appears at a standstill, with Iran saying Washington could no longer dictate terms.

item.title: US conditions for paying UN dues include China curbs, report says
item.description: The US has set conditions for paying UN dues, including curbs on China, according to a report by the South China Morning Post. The exact nature of the conditions and their implications for US-UN relations remain unclear.

item.title: The Small U.K. Agency That’s a 911 for Ships in the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: A UK agency monitors the Persian Gulf and responds to ship distress calls. At a military base outside Portsmouth, England, a 24-hour service monitors the Persian Gulf, the Red Sea and part of the Indian Ocean, responding to distress calls.

item.title: Trump hails US-UK bond as he welcomes British royals
item.description: US President Donald Trump welcomed British royals, hailing the "cherished bond" between the United States and the United Kingdom. The gesture marked a symbolic display of the two nations' long-standing friendship and diplomatic ties.

item.title: Players who cover mouths in confrontations to be sent off at World Cup
item.description: Players covering mouths in confrontations may be sent off at the World Cup. Players who cover their mouths in confrontations with opponents to conceal discriminatory conduct face being sent off at the World Cup - after football's rule-makers voted through plans first revealed by Sky News.

item.title: Charles III warns Trump that allied unity is essential for global security
item.description: Charles III told Trump that allied unity is essential for global security. Charles III warns Trump that allied unity is essential for global security Diari ARA.

item.title: King Charles urges 'reconciliation' in address to U.S. Congress amid Trump tensions
item.description: King Charles addressed the US Congress, calling for reconciliation and renewal amid tensions with President Trump. He noted the two countries have always found ways to come together. In an address to the U. S.

item.title: US regulator to review Disney broadcast licences after Jimmy Kimmel joke about Melania Trump
item.description: A US regulator will review Disney's broadcast licenses after Jimmy Kimmel joked about Melania Trump. The White House is pressuring Disney to fire Kimmel. The move comes as the White House pressures Disney-owned ABC to fire Kimmel after he called Melania an "expectant widow".

item.title: EU Parliament approves common definition of rape
item.description: The EU Parliament approved a common definition of rape across member states. Until now, definitions of what constitutes rape under criminal law had varied significantly across the EU, ranging from "only yes means yes" to "only no means no.".

item.title: Iran war and Strait of Hormuz stuck in limbo as Trump mulls latest Iranian offer
item.description: Trump is considering Iran's latest offer as the Iran war and Strait of Hormuz remain in limbo. The situation is currently at a standstill. Live Updates: Iran war and Strait of Hormuz stuck in limbo as Trump mulls latest Iranian offer CBS News.

item.title: Trump claims Tehran admitted it's in 'State of Collapse' - as UAE quits OPEC
item.description: The United Arab Emirates has quit the Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries, citing a desire to diversify its economy. The move comes as US President Trump claims Iran has admitted to being in a state of collapse.
